title: Ladder of Jacob (Yaakov’s Ascent)
description: A guided study of Jacob’s vision of the ladder connecting heaven and earth, and its interpretation through Scripture and Yeshua.

steps.title: The Vision in Genesis
steps.content: Jacob, fleeing from Esau, sleeps at Bethel and dreams of a ladder set on earth reaching into heaven. Angels of God are ascending and descending on it, and the LORD stands above it, reaffirming the covenant promises.

steps.title: Bethel: The Gate of Heaven
steps.content: Jacob names the place Bethel ('House of God'), recognizing it as a point where heaven and earth intersect. The vision reveals that certain places become gateways of divine encounter.

steps.title: Heaven and Earth Connected
steps.content: The ladder represents a continuous connection between heaven and earth. Divine activity is not distant but actively moving between realms.

steps.title: Messianic Fulfillment in Yeshua
steps.content: In John 1:51, Yeshua references Jacob’s ladder imagery, indicating that access between heaven and earth is now understood through the Son of Man.

steps.title: The Ascent Pattern
steps.content: The ladder symbolizes structured spiritual growth: movement from human understanding toward divine truth through progressive steps of revelation.
